objective: Maximize detection probability under false-alarm constraint using NP energy detection: \(\max_{\eta} P_d(\eta,\gamma)\;\text{s.t.}\;P_{fa}(\eta)\le\alpha,\alpha=0.01\).

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Samples per sensing interval, fixed at 256.

inputs.name: snr_db_grid
inputs.description: SNR sweep in dB from -20 to 0 with 2 dB spacing.

inputs.name: num_trials_per_snr
inputs.description: Monte Carlo trials per SNR, default 100000.

inputs.name: sigma_w2_assumed
inputs.description: Assumed noise variance for normalization and thresholding.

outputs.name: eta_map
outputs.description: NP thresholds for each Pfa target.

outputs.name: pd_theory
outputs.description: Theoretical Pd curves from chi-square survival function.

outputs.name: pd_gap
outputs.description: Absolute theory-simulation gap at mismatch 0 dB and Pfa=0.01.

outputs.name: roc_curves
outputs.description: ROC arrays (pfa,pd) and AUC for selected SNR points.

outputs.name: runtime_ms_per_interval
outputs.description: Measured execution time normalized per interval.

pipeline.name: Initialize constants and grids
pipeline.purpose: Prepare fixed experiment dimensions and SNR linear grid.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.array for dB grid and gamma_grid = 10**(snr_db/10).

pipeline.name: Set RNG and noise reference
pipeline.purpose: Fix reproducibility and define assumed/true-noise mismatch scenarios.

pipeline.name: Compute NP thresholds
pipeline.purpose: Obtain analytic thresholds satisfying each Pfa target.

pipeline.name: Compute theoretical Pd
pipeline.purpose: Generate closed-form reference curves over SNR and Pfa points.

pipeline.name: Generate H0 Monte Carlo samples
pipeline.purpose: Create noise-only intervals under each mismatch case.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use rng.standard_normal((T,N)) for real and imag parts, then combine.

pipeline.name: Generate H1 Monte Carlo samples per SNR
pipeline.purpose: Create occupied-band intervals with Gaussian signal plus noise.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Vectorize over trials and samples; loop only over SNR points.

pipeline.name: Compute normalized energy statistics
pipeline.purpose: Reduce each interval to the sufficient scalar statistic.

pipeline.name: Apply NP decision rule
pipeline.purpose: Convert statistics to binary occupancy decisions for each threshold.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use boolean comparisons and astype(np.float64) for averaging.

pipeline.name: Estimate empirical Pfa/Pd and theory gap
pipeline.purpose: Compute Monte Carlo performance and check agreement with theory.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.mean over trial axis and np.abs difference.

pipeline.name: Generate full ROC and AUC
pipeline.purpose: Produce threshold-swept operating curves and scalar AUC summaries.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.quantile for threshold grid, vectorized comparisons, and np.trapz for AUC.

pipeline.name: Measure runtime and operation count
pipeline.purpose: Report practical efficiency and complexity compliance.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use time.perf_counter and simple scalar arithmetic.

core_assumptions: i.i.d. circular-complex Gaussian noise and Gaussian primary signal model within each interval.
Known sigma_w2 for main NP threshold design.
Single-interval fixed-sample detection scope (not sequential quickest detection).
implementation_constraints: Primary detector per-interval complexity must remain O(N).
No external module files; all code in notebook cells.
Vectorized Monte Carlo generation to keep 1e5-trial sweeps practical.
failure_modes: Normalization mismatch (factor-2 chi-square scaling) causing false theory/simulation discrepancy.
Insufficient trials or poor RNG handling biasing tail Pfa estimates near 0.01.
Noise uncertainty causing Pfa inflation/deflation relative to target.

baseline_methods: NP energy detector with analytic threshold

algorithm_spec.objective: Implement a fixed-sample Neyman-Pearson energy detector for single-user spectrum sensing with N=256 complex samples per interval under AWGN. Set threshold for P_fa<=0.01, compute closed-form theoretical P_d over SNR in [-20,0] dB, and validate via Monte Carlo with consistent ROC and complexity reporting. Include optional robustness and comparative baselines without changing the main objective.
